timeline_html:
Summary. Thitaree shared that her shedding has been worsening for over a year. This long course began after a major emotional stress and has been maintained by ongoing high daily stress.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Major emotional stress (ongoing; started Mar 2026)
  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Mar 2026)

Timeline forecast. Emotional and chronic stress can cause shedding that appears 2–3 months after the initial strain and continues while the stress load remains high. Based on the start in Mar 2026, the most intense shedding would be expected around May–Jun 2026, with stabilization several months later once stress levels ease. Early baby hairs usually become visible within 1–2 months after shedding slows. Dominant driver: Chronic stress, because it is still ongoing and overlaps entirely with the emotional event, sustaining the shedding cycle until it resolves. A resolved trigger of this type would typically calm by late 2026, though ongoing stress may delay that slightly.

Where you are now. You are likely in the onset phase, as the stress-related shedding has just begun to intensify. With continued calming routines and recovery time, the shedding should plateau and then move into stabilization. 🌱
